item.title: Nebraska Atheist Lawmaker, Megan Hunt, Blocks Abortion Ban
item.description: Her parting shot was fucking beautiful. "And if I go to the Pearly Gates and meet your God someday—which sounds great, I hope I do—I don’t think I’m gonna get in any trouble for killing all of your bills who vote for this. I don’t think your God’s gonna have any problem with that. And I don’t think I’m gonna see any of you there either." Sauce: https://onlysky.media/hemant-mehta/atheist-lawmaker-blocks-anti-abortion-bill-pushed-by-religious-extremists/
item.dc:creator: TheRealSleepIsForChumps

item.title: You don't have to like it, but this is what peak humanity looks like
item.dc:creator: LexOdin90

item.title: Update: I ran into the street dog again tonight, and managed to give her the meds! So the remaining tick's should start dying off her within a day. Taking it day by day.
item.description:
The chews smell like meat, so natually she was stoked and ate it right away.
After a few days of hanging with her when I see her, she seems to have more trust in me. She rolled on her back tonight for the first time so I could rub her belly
receipt for the meds from today
dis one a good dog!
my dog and i basically are working our way into the street dog packs lol. here we are outside the ice cream shop, waiting on our order. My obsession with this ice cream spot is such that at this point they are willing to pass me ice cream through the gate even after they are closed :P The middle dog is the dog I'm trying to care for a bit, the dog on the right is a badass that hangs out in the town square all day, and the one on the left is my dog. OVERVIEW I have been in Baja Sur for 2 months, and don't have any plans to leave. The amount of dogs that need help here is sometimes overwhelming. This post is about this brindle dog, but to provide some context, I first posted about another dog a few weeks ago, and that post went viral, and then some imgurs graciously proactively offered to chip in some money to help with that dogs care. I was able to get that dog a blood test, a urine test, flea, tick and heartworm medication and quality meals. I also brought him to my airbnb and bathed him in the sink lol. Ultimately, "owners" came forward and I felt I had no choice but to return him, but was grateful I was able to get him fixed up a bit in the time I had him. Dog ownership doesn't mean the same thing in Mexico to some people as it does in the U.S. and other countries. Anyway, a few weeks have gone by and I am still motivated to help some of these other dogs. I love dogs and enjoy bonding with unwanted street dogs. I'm very grateful for those folks who donated a few bucks here or there, as that has inspired me to keep going. Quite frankly I'll be helping these dogs going forward whenever I can no matter what, but now you have some context on the bigger picture surrounding this post! p.s. i try to post all the updates here, but sometimes i end up posting some to my IG stories that i forget to post here as well. if you're particularly interested in what i'm up to with regard to street dogs here in baja sur, you can check out my ig stories from time to time. my ig is greezy_fizeek ... or click this link instagram.com/greezy_fizeek/ - having said that, i will continue to be posting updates here!
item.dc:creator: greeezyfizeeek

item.title: I'm at a loss for words
item.description: She has been one of my best friends for years. I just can't imagine her wasting away. I can't imagine going to her and telling her what her friendship meant to me... not when I thought I had so much more time with her. I don't know how I can go to work today, I don't know what to do. All I can think of was using this as a way to vent
item.dc:creator: IPlayForBread
